popularStory.headline: Life Sketch of Sir John Wright   B. 1643
popularStory.description:
John Wright was born in 1488 in Kelvedon, Essex, England.  He was the son of the Reverend John Wright and Agnes his wife.

    John married Olive Hubbard in March 1509 in South Weald Church, Essex, …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Wright, Knight of South Weald於1488年7月12日出生在英國艾可凱爾維頓 哈契時，他的父親Lord John Robert Wright37歲，母親Lady Agnes Hunt Kelvedon36歲。他於1508年3月17日與Olive Hubbard結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1551年10月5日在他的家鄉享年63歲，埋葬在英國艾可凱爾維頓 哈契。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Wright, Knight of South Weald于1488年7月12日出生在英国艾可凱爾維頓 哈契时，他的父亲Lord John Robert Wright37岁，母亲Lady Agnes Hunt Kelvedon36岁。他于1508年3月17日与Olive Hubbard结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1551年10月5日在他的家乡享年63岁，埋葬在英国艾可凱爾維頓 哈契。

nameMeanings.name: Wright,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a craftsman or maker of machinery, mostly in wood, of any of a wide range of kinds, from Middle English and Older Scots wriht, wright, wricht, writh, write (Old English wyrhta, wryhta) ‘craftsman’, especially ‘carpenter, joiner’. The term is found in various combinations (for example, Cartwright and Wainwright ), but when used in isolation it often referred to a builder of windmills or watermills. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Wrights
meanings: English: variant of Wright , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Wrightsman
meanings: Probably English (although no longer found in Britain), meaning‘servant of the wright’ (see   Wright  ).

variants.name: Cartwright
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): occupational name from Middle English cart(e) ‘cart’ + wright(e) ‘craftsman’ (Old English cræt or Old Norse kartr + Old English wyrhta), for a cartwright, a maker of carts.
Variant of   Courtright  , a surname of Dutch origin.

variants.name: Wrightson
meanings: English (northern England, especially Durham): patronymic from   Wright  , meaning ‘son of the wright’.

variants.name: McWright
meanings: Scottish or Irish: Gaelic patronymic with the English and Scots occupational term   Wright  , meaning ‘son of the wright’. The name is possibly a part translation of   McIntyre  , which fully translates as ‘son of the wright’.

variants.name: Wrightman
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English wright ‘craftsman’ (see Wright ) + man ‘man’, denoting someone who assisted a craftsman.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Wright, Knight of South Weald was born on 12 July 1488, in Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, England, his father, Lord John Robert Wright, was 37 and his mother, Lady Agnes Hunt Kelvedon, was 36. He married Olive Hubbard on 17 March 1508. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 5 October 1551, in his hometown, at the age of 63, and was buried in Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Wright, Knight of South Weald est né le 12 juillet 1488, à Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Lord John Robert Wright, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Hunt Kelvedon, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Olive Hubbard le 17 mars 1508. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 5 octobre 1551, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Wright, Knight of South Weald am 12. Juli 1488 in Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord John Robert Wright 37 und seine Mutter Lady Agnes Hunt Kelvedon 36. Er hat am 17. März 1508 Olive Hubbard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 5. Oktober 1551 im Alter von 63 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, England begraben.

Lui sposato Olive Hubbard il 17 marzo 1508. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 5 ottobre 1551, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Wright, Knight of South Weald(이)가 1488년 7월 12일 영국 에세스 켈브던 해트치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord John Robert Wright(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Agnes Hunt Kelvedon(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1508년 3월 17일 Olive Hubbard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1551년 10월 5일 그의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 켈브던 해트치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Wright, Knight of South Weald nasceu em 12 de julho de 1488, em Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lord John Robert Wright, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Lady Agnes Hunt Kelvedon, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Olive Hubbard em 17 de março de 1508. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Wright, Knight of South Weald родился 12 июля 1488 г. в местности келведон хач, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Lord John Robert Wright было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Hunt Kelvedon, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Olive Hubbard 17 марта 1508 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 5 октября 1551 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности келведон хач, эссекс, Англия.

Murió el 5 de octubre de 1551, en su pueblo, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, Inglaterra.
